files.content: # galileo-v0-5-gaia A Gaia v0.5 package for Galileo's falling-body thought experiment ## Overview ```mermaid graph LR daily_observation["daily_observation (0.96)"]:::premise aristotle_model["aristotle_model (0.01)"]:::premise medium_model["medium_model (0.64)"]:::premise aristotle_daily_prediction["aristotle_daily_prediction (0.96)"]:::derived aristotle_daily_match["aristotle_daily_match (1.00)"]:::derived medium_daily_prediction["medium_daily_prediction (0.96)"]:::derived medium_daily_match["medium_daily_match (1.00)"]:::derived composite_faster aristotle_model --> composite_slower composite_faster --> aristotle_paradox composite_slower --> aristotle_paradox daily_observation --> aristotle_daily_match daily_observation --> medium_daily_match medium_daily_prediction --> medium_daily_match medium_model --> medium_daily_prediction medium_model --> vacuum_equal_fall_prediction classDef note fill:#f0f0f0,stroke:#999,color:#333 classDef premise fill:#ddeeff,stroke:#4488bb,color:#333 classDef derived fill:#ddffdd,stroke:#44bb44,color:#333 classDef question fill:#fff3dd,stroke:#cc9944,color:#333 classDef background fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#bbb,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#333 classDef orphan fill:#fff,stroke:#ccc,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#333 classDef external fill:#fff,stroke:#aaa,stroke-dasharray: 3 3,color:#333 classDef weak fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#f9a825,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#333 classDef contra fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,color:#333 ``` ## Introduction #### daily_observation ★ 📌 `daily_observation`   |   Prior: 0.90   |   Belief: **0.96** > In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. #### aristotle_model ★ 📌 `aristotle_model`   |   Belief: **0.01** > Model A: weight itself causes heavier bodies to have greater natural falling speed. #### medium_model ★ 📌 `medium_model`   |   Belief: **0.64** > Model B: differences in falling speed in air are caused by resistance from the medium. #### aristotle_daily_prediction ★ 📌 `aristotle_daily_prediction`   |   Belief: **0.96** > Under Model A, heavy bodies should fall faster than light bodies in air. 🔗 **deduction**([aristotle_model](#aristotle_model)) #### aristotle_daily_match ★ 📌 `aristotle_daily_match`   |   Belief: **1.00** > Under Model A, heavy bodies should fall faster than light bodies in air. and In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. are equivalent. #### medium_daily_prediction ★ 📌 `medium_daily_prediction`   |   Belief: **0.96** > Under Model B, heavy bodies can fall faster than light bodies in air. 🔗 **deduction**([medium_model](#medium_model)) #### medium_daily_match ★ 📌 `medium_daily_match`   |   Belief: **1.00** > Under Model B, heavy bodies can fall faster than light bodies in air. and In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. are equivalent. #### composite_faster ★ 📌 `composite_faster`   |   Belief: **0.34** > The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone. 🔗 **deduction**([aristotle_model](#aristotle_model)) #### composite_slower ★ 📌 `composite_slower`   |   Belief: **0.34** > The tied composite should fall slower than the heavy body alone. 🔗 **deduction**([aristotle_model](#aristotle_model)) #### aristotle_paradox ★ 📌 `aristotle_paradox`   |   Belief: **1.00** > The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone. and The tied composite should fall slower than the heavy body alone. contradict. #### vacuum_equal_fall_prediction ★ 📌 `vacuum_equal_fall_prediction`   |   Belief: **0.82** > In vacuum, bodies of different weights fall at the same rate. 🔗 **deduction**([medium_model](#medium_model)) ## Knowledge Graph ```mermaid graph TD context["context"]:::note thought_experiment_setup["thought_experiment_setup"]:::note vacuum_setup["vacuum_setup"]:::note daily_observation["daily_observation (0.96)"]:::premise aristotle_model["aristotle_model (0.01)"]:::premise medium_model["medium_model (0.64)"]:::premise aristotle_daily_prediction["aristotle_daily_prediction (0.96)"]:::derived aristotle_daily_match["aristotle_daily_match (1.00)"]:::derived medium_daily_prediction["medium_daily_prediction (0.96)"]:::derived medium_daily_match["medium_daily_match (1.00)"]:::derived composite_faster["composite_faster (0.34)"]:::derived composite_slower["composite_slower (0.34)"]:::derived aristotle_paradox["aristotle_paradox (1.00)"]:::derived vacuum_equal_fall_prediction["vacuum_equal_fall_prediction (0.82)"]:::derived strat_0(["deduction"]) aristotle_model --> strat_0 strat_0 --> aristotle_daily_prediction strat_1(["deduction"]) medium_model --> strat_1 strat_1 --> medium_daily_prediction strat_2(["deduction"]) aristotle_model --> strat_2 thought_experiment_setup -.-> strat_2 strat_2 --> composite_faster strat_3(["deduction"]) aristotle_model --> strat_3 thought_experiment_setup -.-> strat_3 strat_3 --> composite_slower strat_4(["deduction"]) medium_model --> strat_4 vacuum_setup -.-> strat_4 strat_4 --> vacuum_equal_fall_prediction oper_0{{"≡"}} aristotle_daily_prediction --- oper_0 daily_observation --- oper_0 oper_0 --- aristotle_daily_match oper_1{{"≡"}} medium_daily_prediction --- oper_1 daily_observation --- oper_1 oper_1 --- medium_daily_match oper_2{{"⊗"}}:::contra composite_faster --- oper_2 composite_slower --- oper_2 oper_2 --- aristotle_paradox classDef note fill:#f0f0f0,stroke:#999,color:#333 classDef premise fill:#ddeeff,stroke:#4488bb,color:#333 classDef derived fill:#ddffdd,stroke:#44bb44,color:#333 classDef question fill:#fff3dd,stroke:#cc9944,color:#333 classDef background fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#bbb,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#333 classDef orphan fill:#fff,stroke:#ccc,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#333 classDef external fill:#fff,stroke:#aaa,stroke-dasharray: 3 3,color:#333 classDef weak fill:#fff9c4,stroke:#f9a825,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#333 classDef contra fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,color:#333 ``` ## Knowledge Nodes ### Notes #### context 📋 `context` > This package models Galileo's falling-body thought experiment as a comparison between two explanatory models. It does not treat vacuum falling as an observed fact inside the package. #### thought_experiment_setup 📋 `thought_experiment_setup` > In the tied-body thought experiment, a heavy body and a light body are bound together and considered as one composite system. #### vacuum_setup 📋 `vacuum_setup` > The vacuum case is a counterfactual setup in which the resisting medium is absent. ### Claims #### aristotle_model ★ 📌 `aristotle_model`   |   Belief: **0.01** > Model A: weight itself causes heavier bodies to have greater natural falling speed. #### daily_observation ★ 📌 `daily_observation`   |   Prior: 0.90   |   Belief: **0.96** > In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. #### medium_model ★ 📌 `medium_model`   |   Belief: **0.64** > Model B: differences in falling speed in air are caused by resistance from the medium. #### aristotle_daily_prediction ★ 📌 `aristotle_daily_prediction`   |   Belief: **0.96** > Under Model A, heavy bodies should fall faster than light bodies in air. 🔗 **deduction**([aristotle_model](#aristotle_model)) #### composite_faster ★ 📌 `composite_faster`   |   Belief: **0.34** > The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone. 🔗 **deduction**([aristotle_model](#aristotle_model)) #### composite_slower ★ 📌 `composite_slower`   |   Belief: **0.34** > The tied composite should fall slower than the heavy body alone. 🔗 **deduction**([aristotle_model](#aristotle_model)) #### medium_daily_prediction ★ 📌 `medium_daily_prediction`   |   Belief: **0.96** > Under Model B, heavy bodies can fall faster than light bodies in air. 🔗 **deduction**([medium_model](#medium_model)) #### vacuum_equal_fall_prediction ★ 📌 `vacuum_equal_fall_prediction`   |   Belief: **0.82** > In vacuum, bodies of different weights fall at the same rate. 🔗 **deduction**([medium_model](#medium_model)) #### aristotle_daily_match ★ 📌 `aristotle_daily_match`   |   Belief: **1.00** > Under Model A, heavy bodies should fall faster than light bodies in air. and In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. are equivalent. #### aristotle_paradox ★ 📌 `aristotle_paradox`   |   Belief: **1.00** > The tied composite should fall faster than the heavy body alone. and The tied composite should fall slower than the heavy body alone. contradict. #### medium_daily_match ★ 📌 `medium_daily_match`   |   Belief: **1.00** > Under Model B, heavy bodies can fall faster than light bodies in air. and In air, heavy bodies are often observed to fall faster than light bodies. are equivalent. ## Inference Results **BP
